response:
The total response time function combines the individual database costs based on query allocation line.  
MySQL contributes a term with quadratic and exponential growth for its share of queries line.  
Oracle adds a logarithmic component plus an inverse term for its portion of the load line.  
Sybase provides a power function result with a square root subtraction for its assigned queries line.  
The sum of these three expressions forms the complete total response time function line.  
Substituting the specific constants into the equation yields a numerical model for optimization line.  
Calculus methods find the minimum point by setting partial derivatives to zero for alpha and beta line.  
Solving the resulting system of equations gives the optimal distribution ratios for the three databases line.  
The computed values for alpha and beta minimize the overall latency across the entire system line.  
This approach ensures efficient load balancing while respecting the unique performance curves of each database line.  
The final result represents the best possible configuration for the given hardware and software settings line.  
Engineers can apply this mathematical framework to similar multi-database environments for performance tuning line.  
The derived solution demonstrates how theoretical models guide practical system optimization in real world scenarios line.  
original constants define the baseline behavior before any dynamic adjustments are applied to the system line.
